pi_items.source: Direct HTTP verification - URL returns 200 OK with Content-Type: text/csv, Content-Length: 44225 bytes. File contains standard Titanic dataset with 891 rows and 12 columns (PassengerId, Survived, Pclass, Name, Sex, Age, SibSp, Parch, Ticket, Fare, Cabin, Embarked)

Computed chi-square test using scipy.stats.chi2_contingency on contingency table of Survived (rows: 0, 1) by Pclass (columns: 1, 2, 3). Contingency table: [[80, 97, 372], [136, 87, 119]]. Difference from metadata: 0.00098875696056, which is within the specified tolerance of 2.0.
Computed from chi-square contingency table. For a 2x3 table (2 survival outcomes × 3 passenger classes), df = (rows-1) × (cols-1) = 1 × 2 = 2. Matches metadata value exactly.

Computed p-value from chi-square test is approximately 0.0000000000000000000000455, which is far below the 0.0001 threshold. This indicates extremely strong statistical significance - survival was highly associated with passenger class on the Titanic.

Specification parameter for acceptable deviation in chi-square statistic. The actual difference between expected (102.889) and computed (102.88898875696056) chi-square is 0.00098, well within this tolerance.

privileged_info_summary: The Titanic dataset is real historical data from 891 passengers aboard the RMS Titanic, downloaded from the Stanford CS109 course archive. A chi-square test of independence testing Survived (0/1) by Pclass (1/2/3) yields χ² = 102.889, df = 2, p < 0.0001 (actual p = 4.55×10⁻²³), indicating highly significant association between passenger class and survival. All metadata values are accurate and verified against the actual dataset.
